# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 6

This is turn 6. Turns 1 through 5 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 6.

## Turn 6 — the octahedral Frattini branch collapses

I retained Candidate \(A_2\) unchanged, with \(\omega_2=40491355905\): 

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

This is the Turn-4/5 candidate \(R_0[d_0,x_0^{\sigma_2}]\). 

### 1. The planned “essential vector” over \(GL_2(3)\) and \(2O\) does not exist

Put

\[
B\in\{GL_2(3),2O\},
\]

where \(2O\) is the other Schur double cover of \(S_4\), and let

\[
\chi:B\longrightarrow C_2
\]

be its unique nonzero quadratic character.

An exact normalized-bar calculation over \(\mathbf F_2\), performed independently for both groups, gave

\[
\operatorname{rank}(d^1)=46,\qquad
\operatorname{rank}(d^2)=2162.
\]

Since \(\dim C^1=47\) and \(\dim C^2=47^2=2209\),

\[
\dim H^1(B,\mathbf F_2)=1,\qquad
\dim H^2(B,\mathbf F_2)=1.
\]

Moreover the cocycle

\[
(g,h)\longmapsto \chi(g)\chi(h)
\]

is not a coboundary. Hence

\[
H^2(B,\mathbf F_2)=\langle\chi^2\rangle.
\tag{1}
\]

Thus there is no new Schur-coordinate obstruction to measure: the only nontrivial central \(C_2\)-cover is the Bockstein cover

\[
\widehat B
 =B\times_{C_2}C_4.
\]

For every epimorphism from \(A_2\) or \(G_{\mathbf Q_2}\) onto \(B\), the pullback of \(\chi\) is the fixed character

\[
s=(1,0,0)\in H^1(-,\mathbf F_2),
\]

because \(\tau,x_0,x_1\) map trivially under \(\chi\), whereas \(\sigma\) must map nontrivially. The Hilbert form already computed in Turn 5 satisfies

\[
B(s,s)=0.
\]

Consequently \(\chi^2\) has zero obstruction for every one of the 384 epimorphisms onto either \(GL_2(3)\) or \(2O\). Each epimorphism has eight lifts, all generating because the cover is nonsplit:

\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(A_2,\widehat B)|=384\cdot 8=3072.
\]

Direct automorphism enumeration gives

\[
|\operatorname{Aut}(\widehat B)|=96,
\qquad
\frac{3072}{96}=32.
\tag{2}
\]

So the unique nonsplit central cover of each of \(GL_2(3)\) and \(2O\) has normalized local count \(32\).

More generally, for \(E=C_2^r\),

\[
H^2(B\times E,\mathbf F_2)
 =
 \langle\chi^2\rangle
 \oplus (\chi\otimes E^\vee)
 \oplus \operatorname{Sym}^2(E^\vee).
\tag{3}
\]

Every class is therefore a Hilbert-pairing expression. There is no remaining essential obstruction. The exact marked-extension spectra are the same for \(GL_2(3)\) and \(2O\); as previously, \(m@[N]\) means \(m\) marked classes with raw surjection count \(N\).

| \(r\) | Number of marked classes | Raw-surjection spectrum |
|---:|---:|---|
| 0 | 2 | \(1@[2304],\,1@[3072]\) |
| 1 | 8 | \(6@[6144],\,1@[9216],\,1@[18432]\) |
| 2 | 64 | \(1@[0],\,18@[24576],\,32@[36864],\,12@[49152],\,1@[73728]\) |

This closes \(74\) marked classes for each base, hence \(148\) second-central-layer classes in total.

### 2. Every nontrivial simple Frattini module in these branches is harmless

For the four bases

\[
J\in\{A_4,S_4,GL_2(3),2O\},
\]

every irreducible \(\mathbf F_2[J]\)-module is annihilated by \(O_2(J)\). Thus it factors through

\[
A_4/O_2(A_4)\simeq C_3
\]

or

\[
S_4/O_2(S_4)
 \simeq GL_2(3)/O_2(GL_2(3))
 \simeq 2O/O_2(2O)
 \simeq S_3.
\]

Besides the trivial module, the only simple module is the natural two-dimensional module

\[
V=\mathbf F_2^2.
\]

For the true local group, this module has no invariants. Since the mod-\(2\) cyclotomic twist is trivial, local Tate duality gives

\[
H^2(G_{\mathbf Q_2},V)
 \simeq H^0(G_{\mathbf Q_2},V^\vee)^\vee
 =0.
\tag{4}
\]

Euler–Poincaré then gives

\[
\dim H^1(G_{\mathbf Q_2},V)=2,
\qquad
\dim Z^1(G_{\mathbf Q_2},V)=4.
\]

Therefore every such embedding problem is unobstructed and has exactly

\[
|Z^1|=16
\]

lifts.

For Candidate \(A_2\), I calculated the two \(V\)-valued relation defects as a linear map

\[
V^4\longrightarrow V^2.
\]

There are only two possible coefficient actions: the \(C_3\)-action in the \(A_4\) branch and the natural \(S_3\)-action in the other branch. In both cases the resulting \(4\times8\) binary Jacobian has full rank \(4\). Hence its kernel has size \(2^4=16\), and the map is surjective.

This has a stronger consequence than checking individual extensions:

> Once a base quotient in either the \(C_3\) or \(S_3\) branch has been matched, every subsequent minimal Frattini extension by the nontrivial simple module \(V\) is automatically matched. Such a module layer can never be the first point at which \(A_2\) diverges from \(G_{\mathbf Q_2}\).

The direct exhaustive results over the four current bases are:

| \(J\) | Base epimorphisms | \(\dim H^1(J,V)\) | \(\dim H^2(J,V)\) | Split class: lifts / generating | Every nonzero class: lifts / generating |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| \(A_4\) | 24 | 2 | 2 | \(16/0\) | \(16/16\) |
| \(S_4\) | 72 | 1 | 1 | \(16/8\) | \(16/16\) |
| \(GL_2(3)\) | 384 | 1 | 1 | \(16/8\) | \(16/16\) |
| \(2O\) | 384 | 1 | 1 | \(16/8\) | \(16/16\) |

For adjoining \(E=C_2^r\),

\[
\dim H^2(J\times E,V)
 =
 \dim H^2(J,V)+r\dim H^1(J,V),
\tag{5}
\]

because \(V^J=0\). Writing \(m_r\) for the number of admissible ordered character tuples, the complete tower formulas are:

| Base \(J\) | Allowed \(r\) | \(m_r\) | \(\dim H^2(J\times C_2^r,V)\) | Split raw count | Raw count for each nonzero class |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| \(A_4\) | \(0,1,2,3\) | \(1,7,42,168\) | \(2+2r\) | \(0\) | \(384m_r\) |
| \(S_4\) | \(0,1,2\) | \(1,6,24\) | \(1+r\) | \(576m_r\) | \(1152m_r\) |
| \(GL_2(3)\) | \(0,1,2\) | \(1,6,24\) | \(1+r\) | \(3072m_r\) | \(6144m_r\) |
| \(2O\) | \(0,1,2\) | \(1,6,24\) | \(1+r\) | \(3072m_r\) | \(6144m_r\) |

This accounts for another

\[
(4+16+64+256)+3(2+4+8)=382
\]

marked extension classes.

Together with the central calculation, Turn 6 closes

\[
148+382=530
\]

new marked classes.

Some normalized \(r=0\) values obtained by exact automorphism enumeration are:

\[
\begin{array}{c|cc}
\text{base} & \text{split }V\text{-extension} & \text{nonsplit }V\text{-extension}\\
\hline
S_4 & 1 & 6\\
GL_2(3) & 8 & 16\\
2O & 8 & 16
\end{array}
\]

The computations are reproducible here:

[Central-cover certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn6_gl2_2o_central_tower_certificate.py)

[Natural-module Frattini certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn6_natural_module_frattini_certificate.py)

**ESTABLISHED**: Candidate \(A_2\) has the correct counts for every central \(C_2\)-extension of \(GL_2(3)\times C_2^r\) and \(2O\times C_2^r\); the apparent essential obstruction disappears because \(H^2(B,\mathbf F_2)=\langle\chi^2\rangle\). The unique nonsplit central covers have normalized count \(32\). Proved a uniform full-rank coefficient-Jacobian statement showing that, throughout the \(C_3\)- and \(S_3\)-tame branches, a nontrivial simple \(\mathbf F_2\)-module layer can never be the first discrepancy. Explicitly matched 530 additional marked extension classes.

**GAP**: The official 5402-group verifier has not been run. Within the octahedral branches, the only possible first discrepancies are now essential central \(C_2\)-classes over deeper non-elementary bases. Other tame quotients can also have larger irreducible \(\mathbf F_2\)-modules not covered by the two universal rank computations.

**NEXT**: Develop an odd-index transfer theorem for essential central classes. The first test object should be the nonsplit \(V\)-extension of \(A_4\), but only as the minimal representative of the whole \(C_3\)-branch: restrict its central embedding problem to the index-three Demushkin subgroup and seek a uniform equivariant criterion that closes every deeper central layer at once.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
